title: Pilea caulescens
intro: Pilea caulescens, also known as Pilea, is a type of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in reducing stress, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Pilea caulescens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilea caulescens is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Pilea caulescens is classified within the family Urticaceae, which is a family of plants commonly known as nettle or stinging nettle family. This plant is further classified within the order Urticales, which consists of other plants that also have stinging hairs. The family Urticaceae belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, which comprises flowering plants. Pilea caulescens is a species within the genus Pilea, which is a genus of plants native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. The classification of Pilea caulescens is based on its morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics.
uses: Pilea caulescens is a lesser-known species of the Pilea genus.
